label: Segment
description: Send and manage customer event data through Segment's customer data platform.

form.title: Connect Segment
form.subtitle: Stream events into Segment or manage your workspace via the Public API.

methods.label: Source Write Key
methods.description: Paste your Segment source's Write Key to send events directly into that source. The simplest way to get data flowing — no OAuth, no extra setup.
methods.how_to:
## Find your Source Write Key

The Write Key authenticates event ingestion for a specific Segment source. Each source has its own unique key.

### Steps

1. Log in to [Segment](https://app.segment.com) and go to your workspace.
2. In the left sidebar, click **Connections → Sources**.
3. Click the source you want Anton to write events to (or create a new one).
4. Open the **Settings** tab for that source.
5. Under **API Keys**, copy the **Write Key**.
6. Paste it into the **Write Key** field below.

### Notes

- Each source has its own Write Key — make sure you pick the right source.
- The Write Key only grants permission to send events into that source; it cannot read data or modify workspace settings.
- If you suspect a key has been exposed, regenerate it from the same Settings tab. Anton will need the new key re-pasted.
- For workspace-level operations (creating sources, viewing destinations, etc.), add a **Workspace Access Token** using the **Public API Token** method instead.

fields.name: write_key
fields.label: Write Key
fields.description: Found in your Segment source Settings → API Keys. Scoped to a single source.

fields.name: source_name
fields.label: Source Name (optional)
fields.description: A human-readable label to help identify which source this key belongs to.

methods.label: Public API Token
methods.description: Use a Segment workspace access token to call the Segment Public API — list sources, manage destinations, query delivery logs, and more. Paste a token generated from your workspace settings.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a Segment Public API Token

The Public API token lets Anton interact with your workspace programmatically — beyond just sending events.

### Steps

1. Log in to [Segment](https://app.segment.com) and open your workspace.
2. Click your workspace name in the top-left corner, then go to **Settings**.
3. In the left sidebar, navigate to **Access Management → Tokens**.
4. Click **Create Token**.
5. Give the token a descriptive name (e.g., `Anton Integration`).
6. Assign the appropriate **Role** for what Anton needs:
   - **Source Admin** to manage sources
   - **Workspace Owner** for full access
   - Use the least-privilege role that meets your needs.
7. Click **Create**. The token is shown **only once** — copy it immediately.
8. Paste the token into the **Access Token** field below.

### Notes

- The token is shown only at creation time. If you lose it, you must revoke and create a new one.
- Tokens are scoped to your workspace; they do not grant access to other workspaces.
- To combine event ingestion with workspace management, fill in both the **Write Key** method above and this token.
- Revoke tokens from the same **Access Management → Tokens** page if they are no longer needed.

fields.name: access_token
fields.label: Access Token
fields.description: Generated from Segment workspace Settings → Access Management → Tokens. Shown only once at creation.

fields.name: workspace_slug
fields.label: Workspace Slug (optional)
fields.description: The slug of your Segment workspace, visible in your workspace URL (app.segment.com/YOUR-SLUG). Used to scope API calls.

methods.label: Write Key + Public API Token
methods.description: Combine a source Write Key for event ingestion with a workspace access token for full Public API access. Use this when Anton needs to both send events and manage your Segment workspace.
methods.how_to:
## Combined Write Key and Public API Token

This method provides Anton with both event-sending access (via a source Write Key) and workspace management access (via a Public API token).

### Get your Write Key

1. In [Segment](https://app.segment.com), go to **Connections → Sources**.
2. Select your source and open its **Settings** tab.
3. Under **API Keys**, copy the **Write Key**.

### Get your Public API Token

1. Go to workspace **Settings → Access Management → Tokens**.
2. Click **Create Token**, name it (e.g. `Anton`), assign a role, and click **Create**.
3. Copy the token immediately — it is shown only once.

### Fill in the fields below

- Paste the Write Key into **Write Key**.
- Paste the API token into **Access Token**.
- Optionally add your workspace slug for scoped API calls.

### Notes

- Keep both credentials secure. Either alone limits Anton's access; together they grant broad access to your workspace.
- Rotate keys from their respective pages in Segment if you suspect exposure.

fields.name: write_key
fields.label: Write Key
fields.description: Found in your Segment source Settings → API Keys. Used for event ingestion.

fields.name: access_token
fields.label: Access Token
fields.description: Generated from workspace Settings → Access Management → Tokens. Used for Public API access.

fields.label: Workspace Slug (optional)
fields.description: Your workspace slug as it appears in app.segment.com/YOUR-SLUG.
